description: 정상 케이스 — BERT 논문 (Devlin et al. 2019) 3-source 모두 응답

expected_top_match.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding

arxiv.atom_xml_sample: <feed xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"><entry><id>http://arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805v2</id><title>BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding</title><summary>We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. Unlike recent language representation models, BERT is designed to pre-train deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers.</summary><author><name>Jacob Devlin</name></author><author><name>Ming-Wei Chang</name></author><author><name>Kenton Lee</name></author><author><name>Kristina Toutanova</name></author><published>2018-10-11T00:00:00Z</published><updated>2019-05-24T00:00:00Z</updated><category term="cs.CL"/></entry></feed>

data.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding

publicationVenue.name: North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics

tldr.text: A new language representation model called BERT is introduced, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers, which obtains new state-of-the-art results on eleven natural language processing tasks.

results.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
results.display_name: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding

topics.display_name: Natural Language Processing and Text Mining
